journal_entry.description: Refinance bank loan - extinguish old note and record new note
journal_entry.rationale: A loan refinancing involves extinguishing the old liability and recording the new liability. The debit tuple (0,0,0,1,0,0) indicates one liability decrease (paying off old loan), and the credit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) indicates one liability increase (recording new loan). Both loans are $100,000, so debiting the old Loan Payable removes it and crediting the new Loan Payable establishes the new obligation. This is a non-cash refinancing transaction with no tax implications. Total debits ($100,000) equal total credits ($100,000).

ambiguities.resolution: 'Refinanced' is a conventional term — old obligation extinguished, new one created at stated amounts. Old $100 note liability removed, new $100 note liability recorded.

ambiguities.aspect: whether refinancing fees or costs were incurred
ambiguities.resolution: Transaction states 'old $100 note replaced by new $100 note' — implies direct replacement at equal amounts with no additional costs mentioned. If costs existed, they would have been stated.
Refinancing is a non-cash transaction — old liability replaced by new liability directly. No bank account changes hands, so specific accounts don't affect the entry.
Rate and term changes don't affect the initial journal entry for refinancing — only the ongoing interest expense recognition. Entry records principal amounts only.

output.reason: Refinancing replaces one loan with another. The debit side records the elimination of the old $100 loan liability (liability decrease). The credit side (handled separately) will record the new $100 loan liability increase.

output.reason: Refinancing a loan means replacing an old liability with a new one. The credit side records the creation of the new $100 loan (liability increase). The debit side (not classified here) would record the extinguishment of the old $100 loan (liability decrease). Credit: 1 line for new loan = liability increase.
